title: Derris diadelpha
taxonomy: Derris diadelpha is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. This plant is further classified into the tribe Millettieae and the genus Derris. The classification of Derris diadelpha is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships. As part of the legume family, it exhibits features common to other species within the Fabaceae family.
uses: Derris diadelpha is a plant with a long history of use in traditional medicine. It is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's roots and leaves are used in folk medicine to create remedies for these ailments. Derris diadelpha contains rotenone, a compound with insecticidal properties, which has led to its use in some herbal treatments. However, its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment have not been extensively scientifically studied.

constituents: Derris diadelpha contains a range of active constituents, including rotenone and related compounds. These compounds are toxic to insects and are often used as a natural insecticide. Rotenone has been shown to have antimicrobial properties and can be used to control mosquito populations. The active constituents in Derris diadelpha may also have some antifungal and antioxidant effects. parts: Derris diadelpha is a plant known for its potential medicinal properties. The medicinal parts of Derris diadelpha are primarily the roots and the seeds, which have been traditionally used in various cultures for their medicinal and toxicological properties. side_effects: Derris diadelpha is known to cause side effects due to its toxic nature, which can lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. intro: Derris diadelpha, also known as the "Tahina" or "Tahina diadelpha", is a plant species native to Madagascar that belongs to the Leguminosae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Derris diadelpha contains several bioactive constituents, including alkaloids such as rotenone and dihydro-β-agarofuran, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Derris diadelpha is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
